edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-7B-Think-SFT
edges.description: Olmo-3-7B-Think-SFT is initialized from the Olmo-3-1025-7B base checkpoint and continues with supervised fine-tuning.
description_variants: Olmo-3-7B-Think-SFT is initialized from the Olmo-3-1025-7B base checkpoint and continues with supervised fine-tuning.
Olmo 3 Think 7B SFT is initialized from the Olmo 3 7B Base (long-context-extended) checkpoint allenai/Olmo-3-1025-7B before supervised finetuning on Dolci Think SFT.
The 7B Think SFT checkpoint is initialized from the Olmo-3-1025-7B base model, per the Model Family table on the dataset card.
Think SFT 7B is initialized from the Olmo 3 Base 7B (1025) checkpoint after midtraining and long-context extension before supervised finetuning on Dolci Think SFT.

Olmo 3 7B Think SFT initialized from the Olmo 3 Base 7B (post midtraining + long-context); SFT runs for 2 epochs with 1M-token batches and 32K seq length, and the final checkpoint is a souped merge of two LR-sweep checkpoints.
Olmo-3-7B-Think-SFT is initialized from the Olmo-3-7B (Olmo-3-1025-7B) base checkpoint per the Model Family table that lists Olmo-3-7B as the Base Model for the 7B Think SFT stage.

Olmo-3-7B-Think-SFT is initialized from a long-context (yarn 64k, round-5) post-training base checkpoint that descends from the Olmo-3 1025 7B base lineage (olmo25_7b_lc_64k_6T_M100B_round5 yarn-fullonly).
Olmo 3 7B Think SFT is initialized from the Olmo-3-1025-7B base (post long-context Stage 3) and supervised fine-tuned on Dolci-Think-SFT-7B for 2 epochs.

Warm-starts from the long-context Olmo-2.5 7B 6T-token round-5 YaRN checkpoint (internal weka path); the closest released ancestor in the lattice is allenai/Olmo-3-1025-7B (this internal checkpoint is the long-context base used for the Olmo 3 series).
Olmo-3-7B-Think-SFT initializes from the Olmo 3 Base 7B (Olmo-3-1025-7B) checkpoint per the Olmo 3 model flow (Figure 2).
Olmo-3-7B-Think-SFT is initialized from the Olmo-3-1025-7B base checkpoint per the Model Family table in the Dolci-Think-SFT-7B card.
The 7B Think-SFT checkpoint is initialized from the Olmo-3-1025-7B long-context base.
Olmo-3-7B-Think-SFT initializes weights from the Olmo-3-1025-7B base model (Olmo-core SFT script Olmo-3-7B-SFT.py loads a base checkpoint via the BASE_CKPT argument).

The SFT script initializes weights from the OLMo 3 7B base midtraining checkpoint at /weka/oe-training-default/ai2-llm/checkpoints/tylerr/long-context/olmo25_7b_lc_64k_6T_M100B_round5-...-fb13a737/step11921, which corresponds to the publicly released allenai/Olmo-3-1025-7B base.
Initialized from a long-context-extended OLMo-3-1025-7B base (olmo25_7b_lc_64k_6T_M100B_round5 step11921) — the 7B base after long-context-extension (YARN, 64k), prior to post-training.
7B Think SFT initializes from the Olmo 3 7B base checkpoint (allenai/Olmo-3-1025-7B) and is supervised-finetuned on Dolci Think SFT.
Olmo-3-7B-Think-SFT is the SFT post-training checkpoint warm-started from the Olmo-3-1025-7B base model per the Dolci-Think-SFT-7B card's Model Family table.
Think-SFT 7B is initialized from the Olmo-3 7B base (post long-context).
Think SFT warm-starts from a long-context OLMo-2.5/3 7B base yarn-fullonly checkpoint (olmo25_7b_lc_64k_6T_M100B_round5-...-step11921), the long-context base of the released OLMo-3 7B Base.
Olmo 3 7B Think SFT is initialized from the Olmo 3 7B base checkpoint.
allenai/Olmo-3-7B-Think-SFT is initialized from the allenai/Olmo-3-1025-7B pretrained checkpoint and continues with Think-SFT post-training.
Initialized from a long-context (64K, YaRN extension) variant of the Olmo-3 7B base; the released Olmo-3-1025-7B is the public artifact for this base lineage.
Olmo 3 7B Think-SFT is initialized from the long-context-extended Olmo 3 7B base (Stage 3 output) before supervised fine-tuning on Dolci Think SFT data.
Olmo-3-7B-Think-SFT is the SFT checkpoint warm-started from the Olmo-3-1025-7B base, per the Dolci-Think-SFT-7B model family table.
Think 7B SFT continues post-training from the 7B base checkpoint Olmo-3-1025-7B.
Olmo-3-7B-Think-SFT is initialized from the Olmo-3-1025-7B base checkpoint per the Olmo 3 model family table that lists Olmo-3-7B as the Base for the 7B Think track.
Olmo-3-7B-Think-SFT is the Think-track SFT release initialized from the base OLMo-3 7B checkpoint.
Olmo-3-7B-Think-SFT is the SFT checkpoint produced by supervised finetuning the Olmo-3-1025-7B base model on the Dolci Think SFT mixture.

Olmo-3-7B-Think-SFT is initialized from a long-context (64K Yarn) variant of the OLMo-3 7B base checkpoint (path olmo25_7b_lc_64k_6T_M100B_round5...step11921), which corresponds in the lattice to the released allenai/Olmo-3-1025-7B base.
Olmo-3-7B-Think-SFT is initialized from a long-context (64k yarn) variant of the OLMo 3 7B base (allenai/Olmo-3-1025-7B); the script's CHECKPOINT path points at this LC intermediate.
Think-SFT initializes from the long-context yarn midtraining checkpoint step11921 of OLMo 3 7B Base (allenai/Olmo-3-1025-7B).

allenai/Olmo-3-7B-Think-SFT was warm-started from the 7B base checkpoint allenai/Olmo-3-1025-7B, then supervised-finetuned for two epochs with a learning-rate sweep and a final souped checkpoint produced via mergekit.
Olmo-3-7B-Think-SFT is initialized from the Olmo-3-1025-7B base checkpoint per the Model Family table, then supervised-fine-tuned on the Dolci-Think-SFT-7B mixture.
Olmo 3 7B Think SFT warm-starts from the Olmo 3 7B (1025) long-context base checkpoint per §4.2 ("a resource for finetuning the base model") and Appendix A.6.1.
SFT initialised from the OLMo 2.5 7B long-context base checkpoint (yarn-fullonly, step11921), the upstream OLMo-3 7B base.
Think SFT initializes from the 7B long-context base checkpoint (olmo25_7b_lc_64k yarn-fullonly), which is the OLMo-3 7B base released as allenai/Olmo-3-1025-7B.

anchor_list.excerpt: We train Olmo 3 Think to reason by first generating extended thought sequences and then producing a final answer (Figure 2). To achieve this, we curate high-quality reasoning data (Dolci Think), harness a three-stage training recipe (SFT, DPO, and RLVR)
anchor_list.explanation: Three-stage Think recipe (SFT->DPO->RLVR) starts from Olmo 3 Base 7B (allenai/Olmo-3-1025-7B).
Table pins Olmo-3-1025-7B as the base for the 7B Think SFT stage.

anchor_list.explanation: States Think SFT finetunes the Olmo 3 base model.

anchor_list.excerpt: We train all of our 7B SFT models with a batch size of 1M tokens and 32B SFT models with a batch size of 4M tokens, for two epochs

anchor_list.excerpt: This supervised finetuning step is especially impactful for smaller models, offering an efficient mechanism for acquiring strong reasoning capabilities.

anchor_list.excerpt: We train all of our 7B SFT models with a batch size of 1M tokens and 32B SFT models with a batch size of 4M tokens, for two epochs, with packing, and a 32,768 sequence length.

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-1025-7B
edges.description: The 'verifiable-gpt41' slice consumed during Olmo 3 7B midtraining was generated by GPT-4.1 (OpenAI).
description_variants: The 'verifiable-gpt41' slice consumed during Olmo 3 7B midtraining was generated by GPT-4.1 (OpenAI).

GPT-4.1 (alongside o4-mini) was prompted to generate meta-reasoning thinking traces for math/code capability-targeted tasks (Math/Code Meta-Reasoning leaves) and to distill program-verifiable thinking traces, used in Olmo-3-1025-7B midtraining.
Verifiable-QA midtraining data (verifiable-gpt41) consists of GPT-4.1-generated content used directly as training data.

Math Meta-Reasoning and Code Meta-Reasoning thinking traces consumed by Olmo 3 Base 7B were generated by GPT-4.1 (alongside o4-mini), using Pandalla-style annotations as foundation for capability-targeted thinking tasks.
Program-Verifiable thinking traces in 7B midtraining were distilled from GPT-4.1 (with o4-mini), then verifier-filtered.

Math Meta-Reasoning, Code Meta-Reasoning, and Program-Verifiable synthetic thinking-trace subsets of Dolma 3 Dolmino Mix used to midtrain Olmo-3-1025-7B were generated in part by GPT-4.1 prompted with capability-targeted task annotations.
GPT-4.1 produced thinking-trace generations for Math Meta-Reasoning, Code Meta-Reasoning, and Program-Verifiable used in Olmo 3 7B midtraining.
GPT-4.1 (with o4-mini) generated thinking traces for math/code meta-reasoning capability-targeted tasks (self-awareness, backtracking, etc.) included in midtraining for allenai/Olmo-3-1025-7B.

GPT-4.1 (with o4-mini) distilled thinking traces for the Program-Verifiable synthetic reasoning dataset included in midtraining for allenai/Olmo-3-1025-7B (filtered for correctness via Python verifiers).
The `verifiable-gpt41` shard in the stage 2 midtraining mix consists of verifiable-reasoning traces generated by OpenAI's GPT-4.1.
GPT-4.1 generated the meta-reasoning thinking traces, program-verifiable thinking traces, and OMR Rewrite Full-Thoughts content midtraining leaves consumed by Olmo 3 Base 7B.
Stage-2 midtraining mix includes verifiable-gpt41 synthetic data (a 'verifiable-gpt41' labeled source) — content generated by GPT-4.1.
GPT-4.1 generated thinking traces for Math Meta-Reasoning capability-targeted tasks.
GPT-4.1 generated thinking traces for the Program-Verifiable dataset, then filtered via Python verifiers.
GPT-4.1 produced the OMR Rewrite FullThoughts rewrites of OpenMathReasoning items used in 7B Base midtraining.

Math Meta-Reasoning and Code Meta-Reasoning datasets in 7B Base midtraining were generated using GPT-4.1 (alongside o4-mini) on capability-targeted prompts seeded from existing math/code problems with Pandalla-style annotations.
Program-Verifiable traces (in 7B Base midtraining mix) were distilled from GPT-4.1 thinking on programmatically generated problems, then filtered by Python verifier.
OMR Rewrite Full-Thoughts (in 7B Base midtraining mix) was produced by GPT-4.1 lightly rewriting OpenMathReasoning items for clarity/flow/LaTeX formatting.
The verifiable-gpt41 slice in midtraining mix consists of verifiable problems / answers generated by GPT-4.1.
Stage-2 manifest contains verifiable-gpt41 — synthetic verifiable midtraining data produced by GPT-4.1.
Math/Code Meta-Reasoning thinking traces (consumed by 7B) are generated by GPT-4.1 prompted with Pandalla-style annotations.
Program-Verifiable thinking traces (consumed by 7B) are distilled from GPT-4.1 then verifier-filtered.
Olmo 3 Base 7B's Math Meta-Reasoning, Code Meta-Reasoning, Program-Verifiable, and OMR Rewrite Full-Thoughts midtraining sub-corpora include thinking traces generated by GPT-4.1.
GPT-4.1 generated thinking traces for Math/Code Meta-Reasoning, Program-Verifiable problems, and the OMR Full-Thoughts rewrite used in 7B midtraining.

GPT-4.1 generated thinking traces for the Math Meta-Reasoning and Code Meta-Reasoning subsets, distilled traces for Program-Verifiable problems, and produced the OMR Rewrite Full-Thoughts subset by lightly rewriting OpenMathReasoning items for clarity/flow/formatting while preserving reasoning.
Stage-2 mix includes verifiable-gpt41: verifiable-reasoning data generated by GPT-4.1 (off-lattice).

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT
edges.description: Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT initializes weights from the Olmo-3-1025-7B base checkpoint (declared via base_model frontmatter field).
description_variants: Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT initializes weights from the Olmo-3-1025-7B base checkpoint (declared via base_model frontmatter field).
The 7B Instruct SFT checkpoint is initialized from the Olmo-3-1025-7B base model, per the Model Family table on the Dolci-Think-SFT card (Instruct column shares the same 7B base as Think).
Olmo 3 7B Instruct SFT lineage traces back to the Olmo 3 7B base model (allenai/Olmo-3-1025-7B) via the Think SFT warm start.

Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT is initialized from a long-context post-training base checkpoint that descends from the released allenai/Olmo-3-1025-7B base (gs://ai2-llm/jacobm/checkpoints/olmo3-7b-reasoning-sft-final/model_and_optim).
SFT warm-starts from a reasoning-mid-trained checkpoint of the OLMo-3 7B base (gs://ai2-llm/jacobm/checkpoints/olmo3-7b-reasoning-sft-final), which is rooted in allenai/Olmo-3-1025-7B.

The 7B Instruct SFT run warm-starts from the long-context Olmo-3 7B base checkpoint at gs://ai2-llm/jacobm/checkpoints/olmo3-7b-reasoning-sft-final/model_and_optim. The closest released ancestor in the lattice is allenai/Olmo-3-1025-7B (Olmo-3 7B base release).
Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT, the Instruct-pathway SFT checkpoint, is initialized from Olmo-3-1025-7B (the same 7B base used by the Think pathway).
Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT initializes weights from the Olmo-3-1025-7B base.

The SFT script initializes weights from the OLMo 3 7B base midtraining checkpoint at gs://ai2-llm/jacobm/checkpoints/olmo3-7b-reasoning-sft-final/model_and_optim, which corresponds to the publicly released allenai/Olmo-3-1025-7B base per the OLMo 3 paper.
Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT initialized from the OLMo-3-1025-7B base 7B (gs://ai2-llm/jacobm/checkpoints/olmo3-7b-reasoning-sft-final/model_and_optim) and continued with 2-epoch SFT.

The 7B Instruct SFT run is initialized from the OLMo-3 7B base checkpoint (model_name=olmo3-7b in the OLMo-core SFT script). The local gs:// path points to the OLMo-3 base post-anneal/long-context checkpoint that becomes the SFT warm-start.
SFT stage warm-starts from the OLMo-3 7B base checkpoint (gs://ai2-llm/jacobm/checkpoints/olmo3-7b-reasoning-sft-final), the released 7B base model.

Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT is initialized from the long-context Olmo-3 7B base (released as allenai/Olmo-3-1025-7B); the SFT script loads the OLMo-core checkpoint at gs://ai2-llm/jacobm/checkpoints/olmo3-7b-reasoning-sft-final/model_and_optim, which is the internal copy of the public base checkpoint.
Olmo 3 Instruct SFT 7B is built from the Olmo 3 Base 7B model (allenai/Olmo-3-1025-7B), which is the Olmo 3 7B base checkpoint that the post-training pipeline starts from.
Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT weights are initialized from the Olmo-3-1025-7B base checkpoint; SFT continues training from these weights.
Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT is the SFT-stage release initialized from the base OLMo-3 7B checkpoint.

Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT is initialized from the OLMo 3 7B base checkpoint (allenai/Olmo-3-1025-7B); the script's CHECKPOINT path points at a reasoning-sft-final intermediate that derives from this base.
The SFT job initializes from gs://ai2-llm/jacobm/checkpoints/olmo3-7b-reasoning-sft-final/model_and_optim, the OLMo 3 7B base/midtraining checkpoint released as allenai/Olmo-3-1025-7B.
7B Instruct SFT initializes from the Olmo-3-1025-7B base; the script's --model_name=olmo3-7b indicates the 7B base model line and the README/paper recipe places SFT directly atop the OLMo-3 base.

anchor_list.explanation: Table pins Olmo-3-1025-7B as the base for the 7B Instruct SFT stage.

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT
edges.description: SFT dataset for the Instruct family (math, code, chat, and general-knowledge queries); declared in the frontmatter datasets list and linked in the Stage-1 SFT body paragraph.
description_variants: SFT dataset for the Instruct family (math, code, chat, and general-knowledge queries); declared in the frontmatter datasets list and linked in the Stage-1 SFT body paragraph.

Olmo 3 7B Instruct-SFT is supervised-finetuned on the Dolci Instruct SFT dataset, built on top of the OLMo 2 Instruct mixture with improvements for chat, reasoning, and function-calling capabilities (Table 30 enumerates the prompt sources, totaling ~2.15M prompts).
Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT was supervised-fine-tuned on the 2,152,112-sample Dolci-Instruct-SFT mixture (aggregator dataset combining external prompt sources and new Ai2 sub-corpora).

Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT was post-trained (SFT) on the Dolci-Instruct-SFT aggregator mixture totaling 2,152,112 prompts assembled by Ai2 from external corpora plus new Ai2 prompt sets (Tulu-3 Precise IF, Python Algorithms, logic puzzles, verifiable reasoning, hardcoded data, WildChat with GPT-4.1-upgraded responses, Dolci-Instruct-SFT-Tool-Use).
Aggregator dataset Dolci Instruct SFT, the full instruct SFT mixture (~2.15M prompts) curated from all sources in Table 30.
Olmo 3 7B Instruct SFT is trained on the Dolci Instruct SFT mixture (Section 5.2.2).
Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT is the SFT checkpoint trained on the Dolci Instruct SFT mixture (2,152,112 samples) that aggregates the constituent prompt sources enumerated below.
Olmo 3 7B Instruct-SFT is trained on the Dolci Instruct SFT mixture (~2.15M prompts) covering chat, IF, math, coding, safety, science, multilingual, and tool-use data (Table 30).
Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT was supervised-fine-tuned on the Dolci Instruct SFT mixture (2,152,112 samples spanning math, code, instruction-following, multilingual, science, safety, and tool-use prompts).
Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT was supervised fine-tuned on the Dolci-Instruct-SFT mix (gs path 'rl-sft-32k/olmo3-32b-instruct-sft-1114' is the same data tag used for OLMo-3 Instruct SFT).

Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT was supervised-fine-tuned on the Dolci Instruct SFT mixture, a 2,152,112-sample ODC-BY licensed corpus comprising both repurposed prompts and new prompts authored by the OLMo team.

allenai/Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT was supervised fine-tuned on allenai/Dolci-Instruct-SFT (~2.15M filtered prompt-response pairs) covering chat, IF, math, code, safety, science, multilingual, function calling, and other domains.
SFT data path gs://ai2-llm/jacobm/data/sft/rl-sft-32k/olmo3-32b-instruct-sft-1114 corresponds to the Dolci-Instruct-SFT mixture per the lattice description for Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT.
Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT was supervised fine-tuned on the Dolci-Instruct-SFT mixture (2,152,112 samples spanning multilingual chat, instruction following, tool use, code, math, and safety prompts).

Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT trained on the full Dolci-Instruct-SFT mix; the no-tools variant (1,924,533 samples) is a downstream republish of the same training data with tool-use rows excised, not a separate training event.

Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT is fine-tuned on the Dolci-Instruct-SFT mixture (~2,152,112 prompts in Table 30), spanning Chat, Precise IF, Math, Coding, Safety, Science, Multilingual, Tool Use and other categories.
The SFT run is fed the private GS dataset olmo3-32b-instruct-sft-1114, which is the internal preparation of the Dolci-Instruct-SFT mixture publicly released alongside the OLMo 3 paper.
SFT mixture is the OLMo-3 32B Instruct SFT dataset (rl-sft-32k/olmo3-32b-instruct-sft-1114), released publicly as Dolci-Instruct-SFT for the OLMo-3 Instruct track.
allenai/Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT is supervised-fine-tuned on the Dolci-Instruct-SFT aggregator (~2.15M prompts).

Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT was supervised fine-tuned on the Dolci-Instruct-SFT mixture (2,152,112 samples) — the umbrella post-training dataset that aggregates ~21 prompt sources for the Olmo-3 7B Instruct path.

Supervised fine-tuning data is the 32k packed rl-sft-32k mix at gs://ai2-llm/jacobm/data/sft/rl-sft-32k/olmo3-32b-instruct-sft-1114; per the OLMo-3 paper this is released as allenai/Dolci-Instruct-SFT, the official OLMo-3 Instruct SFT mixture.
Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT was trained on the Dolci Instruct SFT aggregator mixture (2,152,112 multilingual SFT samples) released by Allen AI.
Olmo 3 7B Instruct SFT trains on the Dolci Instruct SFT mixture.
SFT supervised data is the rl-sft 32k mix at gs://ai2-llm/jacobm/data/sft/rl-sft-32k/olmo3-32b-instruct-sft-1114, the internal preparation of the Dolci-Instruct-SFT dataset.
Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT was trained on the Dolci Instruct SFT mixture (2,152,112 samples) - the SFT-stage post-training mixture for the Instruct line of Olmo 3 7B.
Olmo 3 7B Instruct SFT trains on the Dolci Instruct SFT mixture (~2.15M prompts; Table 30).

Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT was trained on the full Dolci Instruct SFT aggregator mixture (2,152,112 samples, ODC-BY) covering math, code, instruction-following, multilingual, safety, science and tool-use prompts.

Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT is trained on the public Dolci-Instruct-SFT mixture (~2.15M samples spanning OpenThoughts 3, FLAN v2, Tulu 3 personas, WildChat, WildGuardMix, Aya, Dolci Tulu 3 Precise IF, etc.); the script loads it from the internal GS mirror at gs://ai2-llm/jacobm/data/sft/rl-sft-32k/olmo3-32b-instruct-sft-1114.
Aggregator Dolci Instruct SFT used as the SFT mixture.

Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT was supervised fine-tuned on the Dolci Instruct SFT mixture, an aggregate of 2,152,112 samples drawn from a curated set of public prompt sources plus new Ai2-authored prompts; counts are smaller than original sources due to extensive quality filtering and Azure API content filtering.
Olmo 3 7B Instruct SFT is trained on the Dolci-Instruct-SFT mixture (2,152,112 prompts spanning chat, math, code, science, safety, multilingual, IF, tool-use, and reasoning sources per Table 30).
SFT data: Dolci-Instruct-SFT mixture (declared in YAML frontmatter datasets and linked in body as 'Dolci-Instruct-SFT-7B' pointing to allenai/dolci-instruct-sft).

Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT is trained on the full Dolci Instruct SFT aggregate mixture of 2,152,112 multilingual ODC-BY prompts, which bundles the component sub-datasets enumerated in the same dataset card.
Aggregator: Dolci Instruct SFT mixture (2,152,112 samples) is the SFT training corpus for Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT, composed of ~20 sub-corpora enumerated below.
Olmo 3 Instruct 7B SFT is trained on the Dolci Instruct SFT mixture, an aggregator dataset whose composition is detailed in Table 30.
The 7B Instruct SFT is trained on the OLMo 3 Instruct SFT mix corresponding to the released Dolci-Instruct-SFT dataset; the script points at a private GCS prefix for this same mix.

Trained on the Dolci-Instruct-SFT dataset; the script references the working path gs://ai2-llm/jacobm/data/sft/rl-sft-32k/olmo3-32b-instruct-sft-1114 which is the pre-release staging path for allenai/Dolci-Instruct-SFT.
Trained on the aggregated Dolci-Instruct-SFT mixture, which is built on top of the OLMo 2 Instruct mixture and adds new function-calling, IF, and science data.
Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT was trained on the full Dolci Instruct SFT mixture (2,152,112 prompts) — the released aggregator for the Olmo 3 7B Instruct SFT stage.
Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT was post-trained (SFT) on the Dolci Instruct SFT mixture (2,152,112 samples).
Olmo 3 7B Instruct SFT is trained on the Dolci Instruct SFT mixture (2,152,112 prompts; Table 30).
Olmo 3 7B Instruct SFT trained on allenai/Dolci-Instruct-SFT (136,833 prompts in SFT mix; Table 30) — subset: Dolci Instruct Precise IF
Olmo 3 7B Instruct SFT trained on allenai/Dolci-Instruct-SFT (186,345 prompts in SFT mix; Table 30) — subset: Dolci Instruct Python Algorithms
Olmo 3 7B Instruct SFT trained on allenai/Dolci-Instruct-SFT (69 prompts in SFT mix; Table 30) — subset: Dolci Instruct Hardcoded
Dolci-Instruct-SFT (2,152,112-prompt SFT mixture) is the SFT training data for Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT.

The SFT trainer reads its dataset from gs://ai2-llm/jacobm/data/sft/rl-sft-32k/olmo3-32b-instruct-sft-1114, which is the working corpus for the Olmo-3 instruct SFT family aggregated as Dolci-Instruct-SFT.

7B Instruct SFT trains on the Dolci-Instruct-SFT mixture (the public release counterpart of the rl-sft-32k Instruct SFT data referenced via dataset_path); per the lattice, Dolci-Instruct-SFT is the SFT mixture for OLMo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT.
Dolci Instruct SFT Mixture (2,152,112 samples, ODC-BY) is the SFT data for Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT. Aggregator-level edge — see sibling events for component datasets.

Olmo 3 7B Instruct SFT is trained on the Dolci Instruct SFT Mixture, an SFT data aggregator that builds upon the OLMo 2 Instruct mixture and adds new chat, reasoning, function-calling, and science prompts (Table 30).
Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT trains on the Dolci Instruct SFT Mixture (Allen AI's canonical instruct-SFT data for OLMo 3 Instruct).

Olmo 3 7B Instruct-DPO is preference-tuned starting from the Olmo 3 7B Instruct-SFT checkpoint (the standard SFT->DPO->RL pipeline of Section 5).
DPO stage of Olmo 3 7B Instruct is initialized from the Instruct SFT checkpoint per the post-training pipeline.
Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO is initialized from the Instruct-SFT 1115 checkpoint (Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT).

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-7B-Think-SFT
edges.description: GPT-4.1 synthesized the test cases used to filter QwQ-32B code completions for correctness in the Dolci Think Python Algorithms code subset of Dolci-Think-SFT-7B; only completions passing those generated test cases survived into the SFT mix.
description_variants: GPT-4.1 synthesized the test cases used to filter QwQ-32B code completions for correctness in the Dolci Think Python Algorithms code subset of Dolci-Think-SFT-7B; only completions passing those generated test cases survived into the SFT mix.
GPT-4.1 generates synthetic unit tests used to filter QwQ-32B-generated code completions for correctness in Dolci Think Python Algorithms (Think SFT 7B).

Code completions in Dolci-Think-SFT-7B are filtered for correctness against synthetically-generated test cases produced by GPT-4.1 (i.e., GPT-4.1 acts as the test-case generator that decides inclusion via execution-based correctness).

For Dolci Think Python Algorithms used in allenai/Olmo-3-7B-Think-SFT's SFT, GPT-4.1 generated synthetic test cases that were used to filter QwQ-32B-generated code completions for correctness; GPT-4.1 thus decided inclusion of code completions.

GPT-4.1 synthesizes test cases that are run against QwQ-32B-generated code completions; only correct completions are kept, so GPT-4.1 acts as a quality filter for the code subset of Dolci-Think-SFT-7B.
GPT-4.1 generates synthetic test cases used to filter QwQ-32B code completions for correctness.

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT
edges.description: Olmo 3 7B Instruct-SFT is initialized from the Olmo 3 7B Think-SFT checkpoint to give the Instruct training a 'warm-start'; Table 29 reports a +3.3 average-point gain for Instruct SFT trained with thinking SFT first vs without.
description_variants: Olmo 3 7B Instruct-SFT is initialized from the Olmo 3 7B Think-SFT checkpoint to give the Instruct training a 'warm-start'; Table 29 reports a +3.3 average-point gain for Instruct SFT trained with thinking SFT first vs without.
Olmo 3 Instruct SFT is initialized from the Olmo 3 Think SFT checkpoint to provide a warm-start; this significantly improves Instruct performance per Table 29.
Olmo 3 7B Instruct-SFT is initialized from the Olmo 3 7B Think-SFT checkpoint as a 'warm-start'. The team found this significantly improves performance (Table 29).
Olmo 3 7B Instruct SFT is initialized from Olmo-3-7B-Think-SFT (warm-start) before SFT on Dolci-Instruct-SFT; this yields significant gains over training from base.
Per Section 5.2.2 and Figure 2, the Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT model is initialized from the Olmo-3-7B-Think-SFT checkpoint (warm-start) before SFT on Dolci-Instruct-SFT.

Olmo 3 7B Instruct SFT is warm-started from Olmo 3 7B Think SFT, not from Olmo 3 Base directly. Section 5.5 reports this strategy yields gains across benchmarks while keeping responses succinct without remnants of thinking traces.
Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT warm-starts from the Olmo-3-7B-Think-SFT checkpoint (Sec 5.2.2).
Olmo 3 Instruct 7B SFT initializes weights from Olmo 3 Think SFT 7B checkpoint as a thinking-warm-start.
Olmo 3 7B Instruct SFT is initialized from the Olmo 3 7B Think SFT checkpoint (warm-start), which significantly improves Instruct performance per Table 29.
Olmo 3 7B Instruct SFT initializes from Olmo 3 7B Think SFT for warm-start.
Olmo 3 Instruct 7B SFT is warm-started from the Olmo 3 Think 7B SFT checkpoint, which the paper reports yields significant Instruct-stage performance gains.
OLMo 3 Instruct SFT for the 7B variant warm-starts from the Olmo 3 Think SFT checkpoint at the same scale, which significantly improves Instruct benchmark scores (Table 29).
Olmo 3 7B Instruct SFT warm-starts from Olmo 3 7B Think SFT (§5.2.2). Table 29 shows With-thinking-SFT-first averages 47.8 vs 44.5 No-thinking-SFT.

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO
edges.description: GPT-4.1 acts as the LLM judge in the delta-aware GPT-judged preference pipeline of Dolci Instruct DPO, modernizing the OLMo 2 / Tulu 3 UltraFeedback pipeline (GPT-4o was the previous judge).
description_variants: GPT-4.1 acts as the LLM judge in the delta-aware GPT-judged preference pipeline of Dolci Instruct DPO, modernizing the OLMo 2 / Tulu 3 UltraFeedback pipeline (GPT-4o was the previous judge).
Delta-aware GPT-judged preference pairs use GPT-4.1 as the LLM judge (modernizing from GPT-4o); the judge selects/ranks chosen vs rejected completions.
Dolci Instruct DPO chosen/rejected pairs are filtered by a GPT-4.1 judge using UltraFeedback judge prompts before training 7B Instruct-DPO (Appendix A.7.4).

GPT-4.1 acted as the LM judge for the delta-aware GPT-judged preference pairs in allenai/Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO's DPO data, scoring multiple candidate responses; the worst response was selected as the rejected completion to maximize the quality delta.
GPT-4.1 is the UltraFeedback-style LLM judge that selects chosen/rejected completions from the four-completion pool, producing the GPT-judged delta-aware subset of Dolci-Instruct-DPO.

Delta-aware GPT-judged preference pairs are produced by sampling four model completions per prompt and judging them with a GPT-4.1 judge using the UltraFeedback judge prompts; the worst is selected as rejected and the best as chosen.
GPT-4.1 judges and selects chosen/rejected preference pairs in the delta-aware GPT-judged subset of allenai/Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO's DPO data.
GPT-4.1 acts as LLM judge selecting chosen/rejected responses with UltraFeedback prompts in Dolci Instruct DPO.
GPT-4.1 serves as the LLM-as-judge that ranks the four sampled completions (with UltraFeedback judge prompts) for the GPT-judged delta-aware preference pair subset of Dolci Instruct DPO.
GPT-4.1 acts as judge filter for delta-aware preference pairs.

(Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO consumes Dolci-Instruct-DPO.) GPT-4.1 acts as the LLM judge selecting chosen vs rejected pairs in the GPT-judged subset of Dolci-Instruct-DPO using UltraFeedback judge prompts.
Dolci Instruct DPO additionally uses GPT-4.1 as the LLM judge (upgraded from GPT-4o) to label delta-aware preference pairs.

Delta-aware GPT-judged DPO pairs (§5.3.1) are scored/binarized by a GPT-4.1 LLM judge (UltraFeedback judge prompts). The judge selects the worst response as rejected and the best as chosen; this filters preference pairs used to train Olmo 3 7B Instruct DPO.

anchor_list.excerpt: Our initial attempts to modernize the UltraFeedback pipeline from OLMo 2 and Tülu 3 by improving the quality of the LLM judge (GPT-4o → GPT-4.1) ... For each prompt, we sample four model completions and judge them via a GPT-4.1 judge with the UltraFeedback judge prompts ... we enforce our judge pipeline to sample responses from exactly two of the following smaller and/or previous generation models which show lower overall performance: OLMo 2-1B-Instruct, OLMo 2-7B-Instruct, Yi-9B, Yi-34B, Phi4-Mini-Instruct, Qwen3-0.6B (no reasoning), Qwen3-1.7B (no reasoning).

description_variants: GPT-4o is one of the multi-turn synthetic preference chosen-side generators in Dolci Instruct DPO (paired with GPT-3.5 as the rejected-side generator).
Multi-turn preference pair chosen completions are generated by GPT-4o.
Multi-turn DPO pairs for 32B Instruct-DPO use GPT-4o-generated chosen completions (Section 5.3.1).
GPT-4o generates chosen responses for the multi-turn preference pairs in allenai/Olmo-3.1-32B-Instruct-DPO's DPO data.
GPT-4o generates chosen completions in multi-turn DPO pairs (32B uses same data).
GPT-4o generator at 32B DPO.
(Olmo-3.1-32B-Instruct-DPO consumes Dolci-Instruct-DPO.) GPT-4o is one of the multi-turn preference pair generators for the chat multi-turn subset of Dolci-Instruct-DPO.
Multi-turn preference completion pairs (self-talk and synthetic-context) are generated either by GPT-4o (chosen) and GPT-3.5 (rejected) or by Qwen3 32B and Qwen3 0.6B.
Multi-turn preference pairs (final-turn chosen/rejected) are generated by either GPT-4o + GPT-3.5 or Qwen3-32B + Qwen3-0.6B (thinking off); GPT-4o serves as the strong-side generator in the GPT path.

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO
edges.description: GPT-4.1 (config 'gpt-4.1-2025-04-14') generated 2,500,999 responses in the Dolci-DPO-Model-Response-Pool — the largest single contributor.
description_variants: GPT-4.1 (config 'gpt-4.1-2025-04-14') generated 2,500,999 responses in the Dolci-DPO-Model-Response-Pool — the largest single contributor.

OpenAI/gpt-4.1 contributed responses (chosen and/or rejected) to the Dolci-DPO-Model-Response-Pool from which allenai/Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO's DPO preference pairs were drawn (source config: gpt-4.1-2025-04-14 snapshot config).
GPT-judged DPO pairs for 7B Instruct-DPO sample completions from GPT-4.1-2025-04-14 (Appendix A.7.4).

OpenAI/gpt-4.1 contributed responses to the Dolci DPO Model Response Pool (configs: gpt-4.1-2025-04-14); these responses were filtered into chosen/rejected pairs in Dolci-Instruct-DPO and so generated training data for Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO. GPT-4.1 (2025-04-14 snapshot) generated 2,500,999 responses (full coverage).

This generator's responses to ~2.5M shared prompts populate the Dolci-DPO-Model-Response-Pool, from which chosen/rejected completions for Dolci-Instruct-DPO (the DPO training mixture for Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO) were drawn. Pool config 'gpt-4.1-2025-04-14' (GPT-4.1 snapshot dated 2025-04-14).

OpenAI/gpt-4.1 (no-reasoning) is a generator in the Dolci-DPO-Model-Response-Pool (config 'gpt-4.1-2025-04-14'); produced up to ~2.5M chat responses to Dolci-Instruct-SFT and WildChat prompts that fed into Dolci-Instruct-DPO chosen/rejected pair construction for Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO preference tuning.
GPT-4.1-2025-04-14 contributed sampled completions to the prompt pool whose outputs are judged by GPT-4.1 to form the delta-aware GPT-judged subset of Dolci-Instruct-DPO.

OpenAI/gpt-4.1 is one of the models in the completion pool used to generate candidate responses for the GPT-4.1-judged subset of Dolci Instruct DPO preference pairs (A.7.4 model pool for LLM-judged DPO preference pairs).

GPT-4.1 (2025-04-14 snapshot) contributed responses to the response pool. Selected responses became chosen or rejected completions in the Dolci Instruct DPO Mixture used to preference-tune Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO.

GPT-4.1 (2025-04-14 snapshot) (config 'gpt-4.1-2025-04-14') generated up to 2,500,999 candidate responses for the Dolci-DPO-Model-Response-Pool, which provided chosen/rejected candidates for the Dolci-Instruct-DPO preference pairs used to train Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO.
OpenAI GPT-4.1 (2025-04-14 snapshot) generated ~2,500,999 responses across the prompt pool — the largest single source in the pool.
GPT-4.1 generates DPO-pair completions in addition to acting as judge.

GPT-4.1 (2025-04-14 snapshot) produced responses (config gpt-4.1-2025-04-14) in the Dolci-DPO-Model-Response-Pool; these responses became chosen/rejected candidates assembled into the Dolci-Instruct-DPO preference mixture used to preference-tune Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO.
GPT-4.1 (the 2025-04-14 release) is part of the candidate-completion model pool used to generate completions for the GPT-judged DPO subset.

GPT-4.1 (2025-04-14 snapshot) contributed responses (~2,500,999 per inference mode) to the Dolci-DPO-Model-Response-Pool (config 'gpt-4.1-2025-04-14'); these responses became chosen/rejected candidates in the Dolci-Instruct-DPO preference pairs that DPO-trained Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO.

OpenAI/gpt-4.1 generated candidate completions (2,500,999 responses, config `gpt-4.1-2025-04-14`) that became part of the Dolci-DPO-Model-Response-Pool from which allenai/Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO's preference pairs were drawn. Role: GPT-4.1 dated 2025-04-14 (`gpt-4.1-2025-04-14` config).
GPT-4.1 (gpt-4.1-2025-04-14 config) generated ~2.5M responses in the Dolci DPO Model Response Pool, contributing strong chosen-candidate responses to Dolci-Instruct-DPO.
(Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO consumes Dolci-DPO-Model-Response-Pool.) GPT-4.1-2025-04-14 is one of the response generators in the DPO response pool (A.7.4).

gpt-4.1-2025-04-14 (~2.50M responses) is one of 29 generator models populating the Dolci DPO Model Response Pool feeding Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO preference pairs; the dated config matches the 2025-04-14 GPT-4.1 API snapshot.

OpenAI GPT-4.1 (gpt-4.1-2025-04-14 config) generated 2,500,999 prompt-response pairs (the full coverage) in the Dolci-DPO-Model-Response-Pool that feeds Dolci-Instruct-DPO DPO training of Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO.

Delta-aware GPT-judged preference pairs are produced with a modernised UltraFeedback-style pipeline whose LLM judge is GPT-4.1 (replacing GPT-4o), with delta-maximising interventions selecting the worst response as rejected.

OpenAI GPT-4.1 (2025-04-14 snapshot): contributed responses to the Dolci-DPO-Model-Response-Pool (config 'gpt-4.1-2025-04-14', ~2.5M responses), which is the candidate-response pool for OLMo 3 DPO preference pairs feeding the Dolci-Instruct-DPO mixture trained by Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO.
GPT-4.1 (snapshot 2025-04-14) contributed 2,500,999 responses (max coverage) to the Dolci-DPO-Model-Response-Pool used to construct Delta-Learning preference pairs for Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO.
GPT-4.1 also serves as a chosen-response generator in the modernized GPT-judged DPO pipeline (data-generator model updated from GPT-4o to GPT-4.1; §5.3.1).

Producer model OpenAI/gpt-4.1 (config slug 'gpt-4.1-2025-04-14') generated up to ~2.5M responses included in the Dolci DPO Model Response Pool, which underlies allenai/Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO's DPO training data. Mode: snapshot 2025-04-14.
The dpo-yolo1-200k-gpt4.1 preference-pair component (125k samples) of the Instruct DPO mix has its responses generated by GPT-4.1.

GPT-4.1 generated the chosen completions in the dpo-yolo1-200k-gpt4.1-... sub-corpus and in the related-query / paraphrase / repeat / self-talk gpt_pairs_filtered_lbc100 sub-corpora (free-text object — GPT-4.1 is OpenAI proprietary, off-lattice).

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO
edges.description: Qwen3-8B contributed responses to the Dolci-DPO-Model-Response-Pool in BOTH no-reasoning ('qwen3-no_reasoning-8b', 2,499,075 responses) and reasoning ('qwen3-reasoning-8b', 2,233,012 responses) modes.
description_variants: Qwen3-8B contributed responses to the Dolci-DPO-Model-Response-Pool in BOTH no-reasoning ('qwen3-no_reasoning-8b', 2,499,075 responses) and reasoning ('qwen3-reasoning-8b', 2,233,012 responses) modes.

Qwen/Qwen3-8B contributed responses (chosen and/or rejected) to the Dolci-DPO-Model-Response-Pool from which allenai/Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO's DPO preference pairs were drawn (source config: qwen3-{no_reasoning,reasoning}-8b configs (both thinking modes)).
GPT-judged DPO pool for 7B Instruct-DPO includes Qwen3-8B (no reasoning) (Appendix A.7.4).

Qwen/Qwen3-8B contributed responses to the Dolci DPO Model Response Pool (configs: qwen3-no_reasoning-8b, qwen3-reasoning-8b); these responses were filtered into chosen/rejected pairs in Dolci-Instruct-DPO and so generated training data for Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO. Qwen3-8B (no-reasoning mode) generated up to 2.50M responses; Qwen3-8B (reasoning/thinking mode) generated up to 2.23M responses; same checkpoint as the no-reasoning entry, run with thinking enabled.

This generator's responses to ~2.5M shared prompts populate the Dolci-DPO-Model-Response-Pool, from which chosen/rejected completions for Dolci-Instruct-DPO (the DPO training mixture for Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO) were drawn. Pool configs 'qwen3-no_reasoning-8b' and 'qwen3-reasoning-8b' (same lattice leaf, two inference modes).

Qwen/Qwen3-8B (both reasoning and no-reasoning sampling configurations) is a generator in the Dolci-DPO-Model-Response-Pool (config 'qwen3-no_reasoning-8b / qwen3-reasoning-8b'); produced up to ~2.5M chat responses to Dolci-Instruct-SFT and WildChat prompts that fed into Dolci-Instruct-DPO chosen/rejected pair construction for Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO preference tuning.
Qwen3-8B contributed sampled completions to the prompt pool whose outputs are judged by GPT-4.1 to form the delta-aware GPT-judged subset of Dolci-Instruct-DPO.

Qwen3 8B (no-reasoning mode) (config 'qwen3-no_reasoning-8b') generated up to 2,499,075 candidate responses for the Dolci-DPO-Model-Response-Pool, which provided chosen/rejected candidates for the Dolci-Instruct-DPO preference pairs used to train Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO.

Qwen3 8B (reasoning/thinking mode) (config 'qwen3-reasoning-8b') generated up to 2,233,012 candidate responses for the Dolci-DPO-Model-Response-Pool, which provided chosen/rejected candidates for the Dolci-Instruct-DPO preference pairs used to train Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO.
Qwen3 8B in no_reasoning mode generated ~2,499,075 responses across the prompt pool.
Qwen3 8B in reasoning mode generated ~2,233,012 responses across the prompt pool.
Qwen3-8B (no reasoning) generates DPO-pair completions.

Qwen3 8B sampled in both no-reasoning and reasoning modes produced responses (config qwen3-no_reasoning-8b + qwen3-reasoning-8b) in the Dolci-DPO-Model-Response-Pool; these responses became chosen/rejected candidates assembled into the Dolci-Instruct-DPO preference mixture used to preference-tune Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO.

Qwen3 8B used in both no_reasoning and reasoning (thinking) modes; same checkpoint contributed responses (~2,499,075 (no_reasoning) + 2,233,012 (reasoning) per inference mode) to the Dolci-DPO-Model-Response-Pool (config 'qwen3-no_reasoning-8b / qwen3-reasoning-8b'); these responses became chosen/rejected candidates in the Dolci-Instruct-DPO preference pairs that DPO-trained Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO.

Qwen/Qwen3-8B generated candidate completions (2,499,075 (no-reasoning) + 2,233,012 (reasoning) responses, config `qwen3-no_reasoning-8b/qwen3-reasoning-8b`) that became part of the Dolci-DPO-Model-Response-Pool from which allenai/Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO's preference pairs were drawn. Role: Qwen3 8B in both no-reasoning and reasoning modes (`qwen3-no_reasoning-8b` and `qwen3-reasoning-8b` configs).
Qwen3 8B (configs qwen3-no_reasoning-8b and qwen3-reasoning-8b) generated responses in the Dolci DPO Model Response Pool.
(Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO consumes Dolci-DPO-Model-Response-Pool.) Qwen3-8B is one of the DPO response pool generators.

Qwen3-8B in no-reasoning mode (qwen3-no_reasoning-8b config, ~2.50M responses) is one of 29 generator models populating the Dolci DPO Model Response Pool feeding Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO preference pairs.

Qwen3-8B in reasoning (thinking-on) mode (qwen3-reasoning-8b config, ~2.23M responses) is one of 29 generator models populating the Dolci DPO Model Response Pool feeding Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO preference pairs.

Qwen3 8B (qwen3-no_reasoning-8b and qwen3-reasoning-8b configs; both thinking modes) generated prompt-response pairs in the Dolci-DPO-Model-Response-Pool that feeds Dolci-Instruct-DPO DPO training of Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO.

Qwen3-8B used in BOTH no-reasoning and reasoning modes: contributed responses to the Dolci-DPO-Model-Response-Pool (config 'qwen3-{no_reasoning,reasoning}-8b', ~2.5M responses), which is the candidate-response pool for OLMo 3 DPO preference pairs feeding the Dolci-Instruct-DPO mixture trained by Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO.

Qwen3-8B (both no-reasoning and reasoning/thinking configurations) contributed responses to the Dolci-DPO-Model-Response-Pool used to construct Delta-Learning preference pairs for Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO; ~2.50M no-reasoning + ~2.23M reasoning responses.

Producer model Qwen/Qwen3-8B (config slug 'qwen3-no_reasoning-8b+qwen3-reasoning-8b') generated up to ~2.5M responses included in the Dolci DPO Model Response Pool, which underlies allenai/Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO's DPO training data. Mode: both reasoning and non-reasoning configs.

Qwen3-8B is one of the models in the completion pool used to generate candidate responses for the GPT-4.1-judged subset of Dolci Instruct DPO preference pairs (A.7.4 model pool for LLM-judged DPO preference pairs).

Qwen3-8B contributed responses to the response pool in both reasoning and no-reasoning modes. Selected responses became chosen or rejected completions in the Dolci Instruct DPO Mixture used to preference-tune Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO.

edges.subject: rl-research/DR-Tulu-SFT-8B
edges.description: DR-Tulu-SFT-8B is initialized from Qwen3-8B and continues training via supervised fine-tuning on 16K deep-research trajectories.
description_variants: DR-Tulu-SFT-8B is initialized from Qwen3-8B and continues training via supervised fine-tuning on 16K deep-research trajectories.

DR-Tulu-SFT-8B continues from the Qwen/Qwen3-8B base checkpoint via full fine-tuning (finetuning_type: full) with the qwen3 chat template; this is the warm start for the SFT stage that precedes the RL stage.
DR-Tulu-SFT-8B is initialized from Qwen3-8B as declared in the HF model card base_model frontmatter; SFT is then run on top of these weights for 5 epochs.

anchor_list.excerpt: We initialize our checkpoint with Qwen3-8B (Yang et al., 2025). For SFT, we run training on one H100 node (8 GPUs) and trained for 5 epochs
anchor_list.explanation: States explicitly that the SFT checkpoint is initialized from Qwen3-8B.
model_name_or_path identifies the base checkpoint that DR-Tulu-SFT-8B initializes from.

anchor_list.excerpt: base_model: Qwen/Qwen3-8B ... This is the SFT checkpoint of DR Tulu, an open deep research agent trained on top of [Qwen3-8B](https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen3-8B).
anchor_list.explanation: Frontmatter base_model field and body text both pin Qwen3-8B as the SFT warm-start checkpoint.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: DR-Tulu-8B (RL) is initialized from the DR-Tulu-SFT-8B checkpoint and continues training with online RL via RLER (GRPO variant with token-level loss aggregation, sample packing, 1-step async tool calls, and KL=0.001).
description_variants: DR-Tulu-8B (RL) is initialized from the DR-Tulu-SFT-8B checkpoint and continues training with online RL via RLER (GRPO variant with token-level loss aggregation, sample packing, 1-step async tool calls, and KL=0.001).

DR-Tulu-8B is initialized from rl-research/DR-Tulu-SFT-8B (the prior SFT release) and continues training via GRPO + RLER preference/rubric optimization; the SFT checkpoint is the warm start for the RL stage.
DR-Tulu-8B is the RL checkpoint warm-started from rl-research/DR-Tulu-SFT-8B; both the frontmatter base_model field and body text declare DR-Tulu-SFT-8B as the initialization.

anchor_list.excerpt: We then further train DR Tulu (SFT) using RLER to allow it to explore and improve both its tool-use and answer capabilities, aided by the evolving rubrics described above.
anchor_list.explanation: Paper states the RL stage is initialized from the SFT checkpoint.
The model_name variable is the SFT checkpoint and is passed as --model_name_or_path to grpo_fast.py, identifying the warm-start of the RL run.

anchor_list.excerpt: base_model: - rl-research/DR-Tulu-SFT-8B ... This is the RL checkpoint of DR Tulu, an open deep research agent trained on top of [rl-research/DR-Tulu-SFT-8B].
anchor_list.explanation: Frontmatter base_model and body text both pin DR-Tulu-SFT-8B as the RL warm-start checkpoint.
anchor_list.position: YAML frontmatter lines 5-6 and body line 20

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: DR-Tulu-8B (RL) was RL-trained on the publicly released rl-research/dr-tulu-rl-data mixture comprising the long-form prompts and the persistent + evolving search-based rubrics generated during RLER training.
description_variants: DR-Tulu-8B (RL) was RL-trained on the publicly released rl-research/dr-tulu-rl-data mixture comprising the long-form prompts and the persistent + evolving search-based rubrics generated during RLER training.

DR-Tulu-8B's RLER stage trains on rl-research/dr-tulu-rl-data, a dataset of prompts paired with search-grounded rubrics (rubrics generated with GPT-4.1) sourced from OpenScholar and SearchArena prompts; the dataset_mixer_list_splits=train field selects the train split.

DR-Tulu-8B was RL-trained on rl-research/dr-tulu-rl-data per the model card datasets field and body link; training script published at github.com/rlresearch/dr-tulu/blob/rl/rl/open-instruct/train_dr_tulu.sh.

rl-research/dr-tulu-rl-data is the released RL training data (4,881 examples per the YAML front matter) used to train DR-Tulu-8B with RL, containing the OpenScholar and SearchArena prompt subsets paired with GPT-4.1-generated search-based rubrics.
DR-Tulu RL data with evolving rubric reward.

anchor_list.excerpt: We focus exclusively on long-form questions for RL training. Following the same LM-based filtering procedure ... we collect approximately 5K new prompts from SearchArena and OpenScholar.

The RL training script's only training dataset is the dr-tulu-rl-data HF dataset, mixed at weight 1.0.

description_variants: Approximately 5K filtered long-form prompts for RL training were drawn from SearchArena (in addition to OpenScholar), forming part of the RL prompt pool.

Prompts in rl-research/dr-tulu-rl-data are sampled from SearchArena (lmarena-ai/search-arena-24k); via the rl-data mixture these prompts enter DR-Tulu-8B's RL training under the SearchArena-rubric subset.

The SearchArena prompts in DR-Tulu-8B's RL data were drawn from lmarena-ai/search-arena-24k (the LMArena Search Arena 24k crowdsourced search-augmented chat dataset); these prompts were paired with GPT-4.1-generated rubrics inside rl-research/dr-tulu-rl-data.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: GPT-4.1-mini (gpt-4.1-mini-2025-04-14) was the LM judge used to score every rollout against each persistent and evolving rubric (returning 0/0.5/1) and to compute the citation reward; its judgement governed inclusion of each rubric's signal in the GRPO reward, hence filtered_by.
description_variants: GPT-4.1-mini (gpt-4.1-mini-2025-04-14) was the LM judge used to score every rollout against each persistent and evolving rubric (returning 0/0.5/1) and to compute the citation reward; its judgement governed inclusion of each rubric's signal in the GRPO reward, hence filtered_by.

During RLER training the rubric reward signal that decides which on-policy rollouts are reinforced is computed by gpt-4.1-mini acting as the per-rubric LM judge (env var RUBRIC_JUDGE_MODEL=gpt-4.1-mini); the judge ranks/scores rollouts against the active rubrics rather than rewriting them.

anchor_list.excerpt: For rubric and citation scoring, we use GPT-4.1-mini (gpt-4.1-mini-2025-04-14) as an LM judge
anchor_list.explanation: Paper names GPT-4.1-mini as the LM judge that scores rollouts against rubrics for the RL reward.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: GPT-4.1 served as the rubric generator G_rubric: it produced both the persistent initial search-based rubrics (after retrieving web/paper evidence per prompt) and the evolving positive/negative rubrics that compare current-policy rollouts during training; these LM-generated rubrics constitute the reward signal.
description_variants: GPT-4.1 served as the rubric generator G_rubric: it produced both the persistent initial search-based rubrics (after retrieving web/paper evidence per prompt) and the evolving positive/negative rubrics that compare current-policy rollouts during training; these LM-generated rubrics constitute the reward signal.

GPT-4.1 generated the per-prompt search-grounded rubrics that constitute the supervision signal in rl-research/dr-tulu-rl-data; DR-Tulu-8B's RL stage consumes this GPT-4.1-generated rubric content as training data, so the relation is generated_by (the rubrics themselves are content produced by GPT-4.1).

GPT-4.1 generated the search-based rubrics packaged in rl-research/dr-tulu-rl-data and consumed during DR-Tulu-8B's RL training (rubric content originates from GPT-4.1, paired with OpenScholar/SearchArena prompts).
GPT-4.1 also generates the rubric items used to grade rollouts; thus it generates training-time signal content.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: DR-Tulu-SFT-8B is the team's own SFT-only intermediate checkpoint used as the RLER ablation comparison: holding tool stack and base data fixed, the RL run gains rubric coverage +8.2, answer precision +4.4, comprehensiveness +5.4, depth +6.5, citation precision +23.3 and citation recall +22.1 over the SFT checkpoint.

anchor_list.excerpt: Comparing DR Tulu (SFT) and DR Tulu (RL), we observe consistent gains from RLER across multiple aspects, including rubric coverage (+8.2 points), answer precision (+4.4), comprehensiveness (+5.4), and depth of response (+6.5).
anchor_list.explanation: Paper presents the SFT-vs-RL comparison as the team's RLER ablation.
anchor_list.position: Section 5.2 paragraph on RLER improves deep research quality

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: Qwen3-8B with the dr-agent-lib search stack ('Qwen3-8B + Our Search') is used as the no-DR-training ablation baseline: holding the inference pipeline fixed at the DR Tulu agent stack but skipping both SFT and RLER, isolating the contribution of the DR Tulu training recipe. DR Tulu-8B (RL) outperforms Qwen3-8B + Our Search by 31.8 average points on the long-form benchmark suite.

anchor_list.explanation: Tables 2 and 3 report the Qwen3-8B + Our Search ablation row alongside the DR Tulu-8B (RL) result.

anchor_list.excerpt: we also evaluated Qwen3-8B and QwQ-32B with naive RAG inference and separately with our inference pipeline using our dr-agent-lib.
anchor_list.explanation: Paper documents Qwen3-8B as evaluated with the team's own dr-agent-lib pipeline, serving as the no-training ablation.

edges.subject: rl-research/DR-Tulu-SFT-8B
edges.description: Comparing DR Tulu-8B (SFT) at 53.9 average against Qwen3-8B + Our Search at 31.9 average isolates the contribution of supervised fine-tuning on the SFT data mixture independent of RLER, confirming that SFT alone yields a +22 average-point gain over the unmodified Qwen3-8B base with the same search stack.

anchor_list.excerpt: Qwen3-8B + Our Search ... Average 31.9 ... DR Tulu-8B (SFT) ... 53.9
anchor_list.explanation: Table 2 reports both rows enabling the SFT-vs-no-training ablation.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: The RLER reward design (per-question rubrics graded by an LM judge) was inspired by the ScholarQA-CS line of rubric-based long-form benchmarks; RLER extends this rubric-grading paradigm with search-grounded persistent rubrics and policy-co-evolving rubrics during online RL.

anchor_list.excerpt: Inspired by the success of evaluating long-form responses with detailed rubrics, including our ScholarQA-CS line of benchmarks, we address this with RLER.
anchor_list.explanation: Blog directly credits the ScholarQA-CS rubric-evaluation paradigm as RLER's inspiration.
